sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.41 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: A subgroup of a linearly orderable group is called relatively convex\/ if it is convex with respect to some linear ordering of the group. Under what conditions is a subgroup of an orderable group relatively convex? { A.\,I.\,Kokorin} Necessary and sufficient conditions are given in (A.I. Kokorin, V.\,M.\,Kopytov, Fully ordered groups, John Wiley, New York, 1974).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
